message:
perf(workspace): statically disable tracing::debug!/trace! in release (OPT-C1)

Workspace-wide wins the 1-2% scattered reclaim flagged in #134 without any
call-site churn: tracing's `release_max_level_info` feature compiles
`debug!`/`trace!` macro expansions to no-ops in `cargo build --release`,
so all 149 debug! sites across 30 crates become zero code. `info!`/`warn!`/
`error!` continue to fire as before; behavior in debug builds and tests is
unchanged.

Why: the INSERT flamegraph still showed scattered debug! call overhead
after OPT-4 removed one explicit site in CellRef::parse. A per-site audit
would churn 149 locations; the feature gate kills them all at compile time
with one line of config.

Verified: `cargo test -p fsqlite-core --test self_connection_read_your_own_write`
passes (3/3); release binary for fsqlite-e2e swarm-multiprocess builds clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
